_doc: Fault corpus for the computation-oracle evaluation. Each entry seeds plausible-but-wrong closed forms ('mutants') for an oracle unit and records the disposition the oracle SHOULD produce: 'caught' = mismatches the enumerated mean at some small n (a faithfulness bug the oracle must reject); 'equivalent' = a different syntactic form of the SAME mean (the oracle must NOT flag it); 'survives' = an adversarially crafted form that agrees on every enumerated n but is wrong beyond the window (a documented blind spot — proof, not the oracle, is what rules it out). p/q are expressions in n, H(.), Fraction, evaluated exactly over the rationals (same semantics as tools/oracle_check.py). Run: python3 tools/fault_corpus.py

mutants.note: forgot the +1 (search of a present key vs the n+1 convention)
near miss: agrees at n=1,2 (1, 3/2) then diverges at n=3 (11/6 vs 2) -- the kind of fault random PBT can miss but exact enumeration catches
(n^2+n)/(2n) = (n+1)/2; the oracle must NOT flag a faithful rewrite (false-positive control)
adversarial: the added product vanishes at every enumerated n (1..8) but is nonzero at n=9 -- demonstrates the oracle is consistency, not proof
the single most plausible faithfulness bug: TAOCP Algorithm M counts running-maximum UPDATES = H_n - 1; records (counting the first element) = H_n. caught at n=1 (0 vs 1)
harmonic indexed at n-1; caught at n=1 (0 vs 1)
near miss: H_n and (n+1)/2 agree at n=1,2 then diverge at n=3 (11/6 vs 2)
H_{n-1}+1/n = H_n; faithful rewrite, must not be flagged
forgot the -1; caught at n=1 (1/2 vs 0)
divided by 3; agrees at n=1 (0) then caught at n=2 (1/3 vs 1/2)
plausible: C(n,2) total pairs without dividing by n slots; E = C(n,2)/n = (n-1)/2, not C(n,2). caught at n=2 (1 vs 1/2)
(n^2-n)/(2n) = (n-1)/2 for n>=1; faithful rewrite, must not be flagged
